text:
Admission Date:  2001-11-30              Discharge Date:   2015-07-20

Date of Birth:  2018-07-25             Sex:   M

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Shellfish

Attending:Angela
Chief Complaint:
Bright red blood per rectum

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
None

History of Present Illness:
Pt is a 62 yo male, borderline HTN, who presents with three
episodes of BRBPR. Pt had a colonoscopy on 1979-06-28 for health
maintainence screening and had polypectomy x 3. A few days ago,
pt started to feel increasing fatigue, but nothing that he
thought was out of the ordinary. Today, when pt made a BM, he
says that he had bright red blood per rectum fill up the toilet
water, unsure he he had a BM. One and a half hours later, he
again felt like he had to go to the bathroom, and had bright red
blood per rectum. No nausea/ diarrhea/ vomiting associated with
this. Pt has not had any chest pain, shortness of breath, or
dyspnea on exertion. He has been able to walk around without a
problem. Christina Montgomery F/C.

Upon arrival to the ED, VS were: T: 95.4; BP: 123/71; HR: 59;
RR:16; O2: 98% RA. Stool was grossly gauaic positive. An
anoscope was done which showed blood in the vault, however, no
active source. Pt went to the commode and had a bloody BM (100
cc of BRB with clots). HR was in the 40s per record (but 20s-30s
per resident in ED). Heart rate noted to be a possible
junctional rhythm but no record of this. During this episode, pt
felt "woozy" and nauseous. Atropine 1 mg was given.
Additionally, 2 amps of calcium gluconate were given for
questionable hyperkalemia as initial specimen hemolyzed with
value of 7.3 (subsequent 3.9). Two liters of normal saline were
given and pt received one unit of pRBCs, premedicated with
tylenol and benadryl.


Past Medical History:
Past Medical History:
Borderline HTN in recent clinic visits
Varicose veins
Mild hearing loss for which he wears a hearing aid


Social History:
Lives at home with wife and a son. Christina Montgomery two sons, one daughter.
Owns a dry cleaning business in Keefe Memorial Hospital. EtOH: one glass of wine
a night. No tobacco or drug use.


Family History:
f: MI at 48, HTN
M: HTN, ? heart disease
S: bladder cancer, brain cancer
B: throat cancer
No colon cancer in family.


Physical Exam:
Physical Exam:
VS: T: 97.1;
Gen: Speaking in full sentences in NAD;
HEENT: PERRLA; sclera anicteric; dry mucuous membranes
Neck: No JVD
CV: I/VI systolic murmur at RUSB without radiation.
Lungs: CTA b/l with good air entry.
Abd: +hyperactive bowel sounds. Soft, NT, ND
Rectal: Tight sphincter tone. No masses appreciated. +mild
amount of gross blood. Gauaic positive.
Back: No back pain
Ext: No edema. DP 2+.
Neuro: Fully intact. Alert, conversant, appropriate.


Pertinent Results:
Admission labs:
CBC: WBC-6.6 RBC-4.28* HGB-13.3* HCT-37.9* MCV-89 MCH-31.2
MCHC-35.2* RDW-12.6 PLT COUNT-171
diff:  NEUTS-73* BANDS-3 LYMPHS-17* MONOS-3 EOS-3 BASOS-1
ATYPS-0 METAS-0 MYELOS-0

lytes:  GLUCOSE-104 UREA N-21* CREAT-0.9 SODIUM-138
POTASSIUM-7.3* CHLORIDE-104 TOTAL CO2-28 ANION GAP-13
CALCIUM-8.3*

coags:  PT-11.8 PTT-30.8 INR(PT)-0.9

cardiac enzymes:
2001-11-30 02:15PM   CK(CPK)-87
2001-11-30 02:15PM   CK-MB-NotDone cTropnT-<0.01
2001-11-30 10:01PM   CK(CPK)-68
2001-11-30 10:01PM   CK-MB-NotDone cTropnT-<0.01
2001-04-09 03:05AM   CK(CPK)-56
2001-04-09 03:05AM   CK-MB-NotDone cTropnT-<0.01
.
EKG: Sinus bradycardia in 50s. Normal axis. Normal intervals.
Slightly early repolarization. J point elevation (less likely
elevation) in V1-V4. No other ST changes. No others for
comparison.
.
Radiology:
CXR AP 2001-11-30-Lungs clear. Heart size normal. No
pneumoperitoneum or pleural effusion.
.
2019-03-07 bleeding scan: no evidence of acute bleeding at time of
scan


Brief Hospital Course:
1.  lower GI bleed - bright red blood per rectum thought to be
12-24 LGIB, particularly after recent colonoscopy with
polypectomy.  Pt has no history of GI bleed previously.  GI was
consulted in the ED.  Pt underwent NG lavage, which was
negative. Patient was admitted to the MICU. Pt given IV
pantoprazole Bon Secours Rappahannock General Hospital, and was transfused a total of 3 units PRBCs
during the course of his hospitalization.  His Hct was checked
q4-6hours and remained stable.  A bleeding scan was performed,
which was negative for acute bleed.  Colonoscopy was deferred
with the thought that he had a post-polypectomy LGIB that
resolved spontaneously.
Patient was transfer to the floor, hct remained stable. Patient
with no complaints, tolerating PO's.
Patient will discuss with Dr Yang whether to re-initiate
ASA prophylaxis

2. Bradycardia - Pt was bradycardic into the 50s, sometimes into
the 40s overnight in the unit.  He was asymptomatic.  Cardiac
enzymes were negative and EKG showed no evidence of ischemia or
rhytm abnormalities.


Medications on Admission:
Medications: None. Pt was on ASA until one week prior to
colonoscopy but did not yet restart it.

Allergies: Shrimp-->hives

Discharge Medications:
None

Discharge Disposition:
Home

Discharge Diagnosis:
1. Lower Gastrointestinal Bleeding
2. s/p colon polyp removal


Discharge Condition:
Good


Discharge Instructions:
Please follow up with your PCP as needed.
Please call Dr Mckee in order to discuss whether it is safe
to re-start aspirin.
If new episodes of blood per rectum, dizzyness, abdominal pain,
chest pain or any other symptoms that may concern you , please
call your pcp or come to the ED.

Followup Instructions:
Follow up with your PCP as necessary.
Please call Dr Mckee on Monday 1979-07-28 to discuss whether
re-start aspirin or not. Phone: 707-149-1452


                             Gabriella Young Mr. Paul Gibson MD 82178

Completed by:1980-09-03
